title: My Presence Is the Work
subtitle: An affirmation for mothers who feel like they are not doing enough
excerpt: An affirmation for the feeling that motherhood requires more from you than you are giving. The counter-statement: your presence is the work. Not the activities, not the stimulation, not the consistency you can't always deliver.

practice.whenToUse: Use on days when you feel like you are not doing enough, not being enough, or falling short of what a good mother looks like.

content.text: An affirmation for the days when the gap between what you think you should be doing and what you are actually doing feels too wide. The activities, the educational input, the consistent routines: these matter less than the culture of motherhood suggests. What matters most is the simplest thing.

The research on what children need most is consistent: a caregiver who is present and available. Not performing, not stimulating, not optimising. Present. The nervous system of a child regulates in the company of a calm adult nervous system. Your presence does the work even when you are doing nothing visible.

Say it on the hard days. Say it when you are sitting in the same room on your phone because you have nothing left. Say it when the day was mainly survival. Say it when you showed up at all. That is the work. You are doing it.
